mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits lower discount rates and easier financial conditions into equities, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when the policy rate is already near the zero lower bound because additional cuts are then interpreted as continued accommodation rather than recession panic.
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle near the zero lower bound
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX forward returns are negative in a majority of months when FEDFUNDS is falling, or if the sign of the relation turns negative after controlling for contemporaneous inflation and labor data.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals weakening household income and consumption, which compresses expected earnings and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is not a generic level effect; it activates when unemployment accelerates sharply rather than drifting sideways.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the average forward return after a 0.3pp/3m unemployment jump is non-negative.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rebound in RSAFS and HOUST indicates broad demand stabilization in retail spending and housing activity, which improves near-term revenue expectations for cyclicals and supports positive S&P 500 performance over the next quarter. The mechanism fails if only one series improves while the other remains weak.
failure_condition: Reject if joint improvements in RSAFS and HOUST do not outperform single-series signals, or if 60-day forward SPX returns are not positive after both series rise on a 3-month basis.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in INDPRO relative to its recent level signals industrial contraction and lower earnings momentum for cyclically exposed firms, which should push S&P 500 returns lower over the following month. The signal is strongest when the decline is broad rather than a one-month noise event.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls by more than 0.8% over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if monthly industrial production drops of this size are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the effect disappears when the drop persists for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is falling while FEDFUNDS remains pinned near zero, the market reads disinflation plus accommodation as a supportive real-rate regime, which lifts equity valuations over the next 20 trading days. The claim fails if disinflation is paired with rising policy rates or if inflation drops are driven by severe demand collapse.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change falls below 1.0% while FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%
regime_hint: disinflation with policy at the effective lower bound
failure_condition: Reject if the triggered disinflation-plus-low-rate regime does not deliver positive average 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign flips negative when CPIAUCSL is falling but FEDFUNDS is above 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A continued decline in PAYEMS signals labor-market contraction and weaker aggregate income, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next month as earnings expectations are revised down. The effect is expected to be monotonic when employment losses persist across multiple releases.
failure_condition: Reject if rolling 20-day SPX returns are not negative on average during 2-month PAYEMS declines, or if repeated payroll losses are followed by positive forward returns more than half the time.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized improvement in GDPC1 and RSAFS marks broad final-demand acceleration, which should raise expected corporate sales and generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The mechanism fails if GDP rises only because of inventory or statistical carry-through while retail sales remain weak.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day SPX returns are not positive following joint quarter-over-quarter increases in GDPC1 and month-over-month gains in RSAFS, or if the joint signal underperforms either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: After a housing trough, a rebound in HOUST signals stabilization in interest-sensitive demand and future construction activity, which supports equities over the next month through improved growth expectations. This signal is conditional on housing having already contracted materially; without a prior slump, new housing starts do not carry the same bullish information.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises month-over-month after falling for at least 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if post-trough HOUST rebounds fail to precede positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal is positive even when HOUST had not previously fallen for at least 3 months.
